_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Beginning to analyze why the categories and tags menu items were removed from the impeccable theme.

Categories and Tags were promoted to the main navigation in April 2025. They were then demoted back to the footer in June 2026. An Impeccable critique from June 2026 recommended this change. It viewed the main navigation taxonomy as excessive chrome and assumed blog taxonomy literacy.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — it was intentional, and it came from the Impeccable homepage pass, not from Hugo or the theme breaking anything.

## Why they were removed

The Impeccable critique flagged **Categories** and **Tags** as **“blog-admin nav”** on a site that `docs/PRODUCT.md` positions as a **personal brand for potential clients**, not a blog archive.

From the June 18 critique:

> **Categories + Tags in main nav** (5 items) plus search + theme switcher = 7+ chrome decisions before content. Blog taxonomy literacy assumed.
>
> **Fix:** Demote taxonomies to footer or blog subnav. Keep Home / About / Work or Blog / Contact.

The follow-up critique still called out Categories/Tags in the **footer** as residual blog-admin chrome. The latest menu change (commit `90e9a87`, June 19) removed them from both header and footer.

## What changed

**Before:** Home · About · Blog · **Categories** · **Tags**  
**After:** Home · About · Blog (+ Get in touch CTA)

Footer went from About · Categories · Tags → About · Blog · Contact.

## They’re not gone from the site

Taxonomies still work:

- `/categories` and `/tags` still exist as Hugo taxonomy pages
- Blog sidebar widgets still show them (`params.toml`: `sidebar = ["categories", "tags"]`)
- Search still surfaces categories and tags (`show_tags` / `show_categories` are enabled)

So this was an **IA / positioning** choice: fewer nav items, less “blog CMS” feel, more “hire this person” framing.

If you want them back, the usual compromise Impeccable suggested is **footer or blog-context only** — not top-level header nav. I can restore them that way if you want.

---

_**User**_

have them appear in the footer

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Two approaches are being weighed. One adds content files for the taxonomy list pages and updates basic-seo. The other approach is still under consideration.

The second approach is chosen. Updating `basic-seo.html` alone is cleaner than adding separate content files. The partial will handle both taxonomy list pages and term pages. List pages will use "Page | JoRap Notes". Term pages will use "Term | Taxonomy | JoRap Notes". This follows the existing site pattern.

List titles will be "Categories | JoRap Notes" and "Tags | JoRap Notes". Term titles will include the specific term name. Meta descriptions were considered but skipped since only meta_title was requested. The content-files approach is being checked against Hugo template guidance.

The implementation will use kind-based logic in `basic-seo.html`. It checks `.Params.meta_title` first, then `.Params.title`, then falls back to the taxonomy singular name for term pages.

The title format is now defined. Term pages will use "Title | Taxonomy | Site Title". List pages will use "Title | Site Title". The `.Title` variable needs the `title` filter to match the display casing used elsewhere. The taxonomy singular name will also be capitalized for consistency.
</details></think>

Taxonomy pages fall back to the site title because they have no front matter. I'll update the SEO partial to set proper titles for taxonomy list and term pages.

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed. Taxonomy pages had no front matter, so they were falling back to the bare site title (`JoRap Notes`).

Updated `layouts/_partials/basic-seo.html` to set titles by page kind:

| Page | Title |
|------|-------|
| `/categories` | Categories \| JoRap Notes |
| `/tags` | Tags \| JoRap Notes |
| `/categories/food` | Food \| Categories \| JoRap Notes |
| `/tags/hugo` | Hugo \| Tags \| JoRap Notes |

This matches the pattern used on About, Blog, and Contact. Pages with explicit `meta_title` in front matter are unchanged.
